title: Building the Best Biscuit Tea ever!
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"63bj","text":"Instructions for how to make the the best cup of tea - and it's not just any tea - it's a biscuit FLAVOURED tea paired with the best biscuit ever. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"19ns2","text":"Be careful when pouring boiling hot water, carrying your mug of tea, and ensure it has cooled a bit before you drink it.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"1s6u7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8fn8v","text":"Water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3d0b6","text":"Water kettle","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8sbk2","text":"Your favourite mug","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5lkfg","text":"Biscuit Tea teabags","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"alnfo","text":"Non-dairy milk with high fat content, ideally the barista versions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6uvu1","text":"Spoon","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"74i6b","text":"M\u0026S Dark Chocolate Digestive","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71jgn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8fkio","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fill kettle with water & switch on to boil (ideally water should reach 100 °C ) Boil water in the kettle If you do not have a kettle, you can boil water in a pot on a stove (be careful not to leave it to boil dry). Or I have seen people sometimes boil water in a mug in the microwave - not my ideal approach, but it would also work. Remember to always close the lid as to not steam your kitchen up.
While kettle heats up, place a Biscuit Tea tea-bag in your favourite mug (extra points for using a Starbucks City Mug from their Collections) Put tea-bag into mug
Pour boiling water over the tea-bag in your mug until about 2cm from the top of the mug Make sure you watch the video before trying at home!
Leave the tea-bag to steep in the hot water for 00:05:00

Add some non-dairy milk to your mug (about 0.5 cm). the best milk to use is oat, but if you have soy or almond on hand this will do. Just nothing too flavourful as to not overwhelm the taste of the biscuit teabag

Use the spoon to remove the tea bag from the mug if you squeeze the teabag on the side you will make it more bitter. I would advise in order to still taste the subtle biscuit brew you should drip dry the teabag and quickly move to the bin. Remove tea-bag
Use spoon to stir your tea: if you want to stir the tea most efficiently you might find the following video on the fluid mechanics of stirirng tea relevant.
Wait for your tea to cool to a temperature that you can safely drink it at
Drink your tea! tea should be a warm golden beige colour, should smell subtly of biscuit. Drink and enjoy
creator: georgia whitton
